zh.Coverham Abbey, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾可福哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ivetta De Ros Scrope於1285年出生在英國越可爾英格曼東普時，她的父親Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp43歲，母親Eustacia Fitzhugh35歲。她於大約1306年與Sir Geoffrey le Scrope在英國越可爾馬斯哈姆結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1331年在英國越可爾享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾可福哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ivetta De Ros Scrope于1285年出生在英国越可爾英格曼東普时，她的父亲Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp43岁，母亲Eustacia Fitzhugh35岁。她于大约1306年与Sir Geoffrey le Scrope在英国越可爾馬斯哈姆结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1331年在英国越可爾享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾可福哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Ivetta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Ivatt
meanings: English: relationship name from a pet form of the Old French personal name Iva, feminine form of Ivo. Compare Ive .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Ivetta De Ros Scrope was born in 1285, in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, was 43 and her mother, Eustacia Fitzhugh, was 35. She married Sir Geoffrey le Scrope about 1306, in Masham, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1331, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 46, and was buried in Coverham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ivetta De Ros Scrope est née en 1285, à Ingmanthorpe, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Eustacia Fitzhugh, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Geoffrey le Scrope environ 1306, à Masham, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1331, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterrée à Coverham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ivetta De Ros Scrope 1285 in Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp 43 und ihre Mutter Eustacia Fitzhugh 35. Sie hat um 1306 in Masham, Yorkshire, England Sir Geoffrey le Scrope geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1331 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Yorkshire, England und wurde in Coverham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ivetta De Ros Scrope è nata il nel 1285 nel Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, aveva 43 anni e sua madre, Eustacia Fitzhugh, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato Sir Geoffrey le Scrope circa 1306, nel Masham, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1331, nel Yorkshire, England, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolta a Coverham, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ivetta De Ros Scropeが1285年にイギリスヨークシュアイアイングマンソルペで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorpは43歳，母Eustacia Fitzhughは35歳であった。約1306年にイギリスヨークシュアイアマシャムでSir Geoffrey le Scropeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1331年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアコバーハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ivetta De Ros Scrope(이)가 1285년 영국 요르크실르 잉먼소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eustacia Fitzhugh(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 대략 1306년 영국 요르크실르 마샤에서 Sir Geoffrey le Scrope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1331년 영국 요르크실르에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 커버함에 묻혔다.

pt.1331: de 9 de janeiro de 1331 a 8 de janeiro de 1332
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ivetta De Ros Scrope nasceu em 1285, em Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Eustacia Fitzhugh, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Geoffrey le Scrope aproximadamente 1306, em Masham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1331, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 46 anos, e foi sepultada em Coverham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ivetta De Ros Scrope родилась в 1285 г. в местности ингмантаурп, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Eustacia Fitzhugh, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Geoffrey le Scrope около 1306 г. в местности машам, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1331 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет и была похоронена в местности ковирам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ivetta De Ros Scrope nació en 1285, en Ingmanthorpe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Ros II of Ingmanthorp, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Eustacia Fitzhugh, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Sir Geoffrey le Scrope aproximadamente 1306, en Masham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1331, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultada en Coverham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
